steps.title: Identify what x and f(x) represent
steps.content: From the problem context, $f(x) = 9,000(1+0.03)^x$ gives the value of the CD in dollars, where $x$ is the number of years after the investment was made.

steps.title: Interpret f(2) using function notation
steps.content: When we write $f(2)$, we substitute $x = 2$ into the function. Since $x$ represents the number of years, $f(2)$ gives the value of the CD exactly 2 years after the initial investment. The problem states this value is approximately 9,474.08 dollars.

steps.title: Match to the correct interpretation
steps.content:
Choice A refers to 2% interest, but the function uses 3% (since $1 + 0.03 = 1.03$) — incorrect.
Choice B refers to days and doubling — doesn't match what we found — incorrect.
Choice D says 9,474.08 is how much more than the initial value (a gain), but 9,474.08 is the total value, not the gain amount — incorrect.
Choice C states: 2 years after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 9,474.08 dollars. This is exactly what $f(2)$ represents. That matches choice C — C is correct.
